title: Instrument axum middleware, delivery worker, and SQLx for metrics
description:
## What
Wire metric recording into axum Tower middleware (request count + latency per route), the delivery worker (queue depth gauge on every tick), and SQLx query execution via a custom executor logger.

## Why
Raw metric registration is useless without instrumentation at every measurement point; this task makes the counters actually reflect real traffic.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read Tower middleware docs for writing a custom layer/service wrapper; read sqlx BeforeQuery/AfterQuery hooks or executor wrapper patterns; read delivery worker source to identify the poll tick location.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: implement a Tower `Layer` + `Service` that wraps each request, records method/route-template/status labels to `http_requests_total` counter and `http_request_duration_seconds` histogram; add the middleware to the main axum router; in the delivery worker tick, query `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM delivery_queue WHERE dead_at IS NULL` and `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM delivery_queue WHERE dead_at IS NOT NULL` and update the two gauges; wrap SQLx query execution to record per-query duration to `sqlite_query_duration_seconds` histogram with a `query` label set to a sanitized query template (not user data).
- Spawn a Test subagent to: fire exactly 10 GET /api/v1/timelines/home requests, scrape /metrics, parse output, assert `http_requests_total{method="GET",path="/api/v1/timelines/home",status="200"}` equals 10; assert histogram `_count` field also equals 10.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: manually count requests sent vs counter value in scraped text; no code-under-test self-reference.
- Test approach: deterministic request count, scrape, parse text exposition format with a simple line scanner, assert numeric equality.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Route label must use axum MatchedPath (the template string), never the raw request URI — prevents label cardinality explosion from post IDs in URLs
- SQLx label must be a static query name or truncated template, never interpolated user input
- Duration histogram must use seconds (not ms) to match Prometheus convention
- Gauge updates in delivery worker must not block the async executor — use `metrics::gauge!` which is non-blocking
